title: campanula rapunculus subsp. lambertiana
intro: Campanula rapunculus subsp. lambertiana, also known as rampion, is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, rampion has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and supporting respiratory health. Rampion can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusions, teas, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
